data.title: Toast, Uber announce expanded integration
data.description:
Toast (NYSE:TOST) and Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) have entered an expanded partnership to help restaurants reduce costs and reach more diners with commission-free delivery.

The companies have been partnering since 2021 to deliver orders made through the Uber Eats app.

The expanded pact introduces a new integration between Toast Delivery Services and Uber Direct, enabling restaurants across the U.S. to save on delivery fees, expand their delivery radius, and leverage Uber's delivery network to offer local delivery of orders made by phone and through Toast digital ordering channels.

In addition to providing restaurants with more choice, this expanded integration with Toast will enable restaurants to access commission-free delivery for a low flat fee per order.

Uber Direct is now available on Toast Delivery Services for customers to fulfill deliveries of orders made by phone and through Toast digital ordering channels.

data.title: Southwest Airlines raises Q4 revenue per unit on encouraging travel trends
data.description:
Shares of Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) are moving higher in Thursday’s premarket action after the carrier raised its unit revenue per available seat mile for the current quarter, thanks to strong travel trends and effective efficiency measures.

“[Southwest] is encouraged by recent revenue trends and forward bookings, including fourth quarter holiday travel, and currently expects strong revenue trends and tactical initiative performance to carry into 2025,” the company said in a filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Unit revenue is now expected to increase by 5.5% to 7% from initial estimates of up 3.5% to up 5.5%. This corresponds to a ~4% decline in capacity on a year-over-year basis.

On Monday, Southwest (LUV) said it delivered a “stellar” Thanksgiving period performance (Nov 25 to Dec 1), with nearly 27K flights flow with 3.5M passengers. And after the Christmas meltdown of 2022, the carrier was proud to announce that it took the top spot among domestic carriers for on-time performance and had the fewest delays of 45 minutes or more.

According to the Transportation Security Administration, this Thanksgiving weekend set another record with more than 3 million people traveling home on Sunday, breaking the previous holiday record by ~74,000.

Southwest (LUV) shares are up 2.8% and set to open at the highest level in 9 months.

data.title: Arqit Quantum reports FY results
data.description:
• Arqit Quantum press release (NASDAQ:ARQQ): FY revenue of $293,000.
  • Loss before tax from continuing operations was $23.9 million

data.title: VEON enters into partnership with Engro to boost telecommunications infrastructure
data.description:
• VEON (NASDAQ:VEON) said it has entered into a strategic partnership with Engro for the pooling and management of its infrastructure assets, starting in Pakistan.
  • In the first phase, VEON’s infrastructure assets under Deodar, will vest into Engro's unit through a scheme of arrangement.
  • VEON’s digital operator Jazz will continue to lease Deodar’s infrastructure for the provision of nationwide mobile voice and data services under a long-term partnership agreement.
  • "This strategic partnership is a pivotal move in advancing VEON's asset-light strategy. The traditional vertically integrated telco model is no longer viable for operators, neither is it conducive to the sustainable growth of developing economies," said Kaan Terzioglu, VEON Group CEO and Chairman of Jazz Board.

data.title: Talen Energy announces $600M credit facility expansion
data.description:
• Talen Energy (NASDAQ:TLN) has launched an incremental $600M Term Loan B credit facility that will increase the principal balance under its credit facilities from $859M to ~$1.459B.
  • The company also expects to replace its existing Term Loan C facility with a new letter of credit facility.
  • Talen (TLN) has concurrently executed an agreement to repurchase at least $600M in aggregate purchase price of shares of its outstanding common stock from affiliates of Rubric Capital Management. This transaction is incremental to the company’s previously announced share repurchase program.
  • That program will have approximately $1.2B of remaining capacity available through 2026 after the company closes the repurchase.
  • The proceeds from the financing will be used, together with cash on hand, to fund the repurchase, and both transactions are expected to close before Dec. 31, 2024.

data.title: Tradeweb reports 30.5% Y/Y increase in November average daily volume
data.description:
• Electronic marketplaces operator Tradeweb Markets (NASDAQ:TW) reported November trading volume of $48.8T, and average daily volume of $2.35T, up 30.5% from last year.
  • TW shares were trading -0.41% Thursday pre-market at $133.81.
  • U.S. government bond ADV was up 50.3% Y/Y to $246B, while the European government bond ADV increased 18.7% to $53.1B. Mortgage ADV rose 22.5% from last year to $230.6B.
  • Fully electronic U.S. credit ADV increased 20.8% to $7.4B and European credit ADV was up 8.7% to $2.5B. Municipal bonds ADV was down 13.8% to $409M. Credit derivatives ADV rose 37.2% to $13.5B.
  • U.S. ETF ADV increased 21.9% on a yearly basis to $8.6B and European ETF ADV rose 29.2% to $3.3B.
  • Repo ADV increased 31.4% from last year to $702.5B.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: TD Bank stock slips after suspending financial targets on strategic review; Q4 earnings miss
data.description:
Toronto-Dominion Bank (NYSE:TD) stock dropped 3.2% in Thursday premarket trading after the Canadian bank said it's suspending its medium-term financial targets as it undertakes a strategic review of its organic opportunities and priorities, productivity and efficiency initiatives, and capital allocation alternatives. Still, the bank increased its quarterly dividend to C$1.05 per share from its prior dividend of C$1.02.

The company's fiscal Q4 earnings missed the consensus estimate as the company implements measures to shore up its anti-money laundering controls. "Remediation is our number one priority, and we continue to make meaningful progress in addressing the failures," said CEO Bharat Masrani.

The bank expects that expense growth for the 2025 fiscal year will be in the range of 5%-7%.

TD Bank (NYSE:TD) expects to update its medium-term financial targets in H2 2025.

"This quarter, we delivered higher fee income in our markets-related businesses, volume growth in Canada, and stable deposits in the U.S.," Masrani said. "A key development this quarter was the resolution of our U.S. AML matters, bringing important clarity to our stakeholders."

In October, the company agreed to pay more than $3B to settle investigations into its AML practices. The settlement also makes TD Bank (NYSE:TD) subject to an asset cap of US $434B on the total assets of its two U.S. subsidiaries.

Fiscal Q4 adjusted EPS of C$1.72 (US$1.22), trailing the consensus of C$1.81, fell from C$2.05 in Q3 and C$1.82 in last year's Q4.

Total adjusted revenue of C$14.9B (US$10.6B) for the quarter ended Oct. 31, 2024, dropped from C$14.2B in the previous quarter and C$13.2B a year ago.

Net interest income of C$7.94B, exceeding the C$7.75B Visible Alpha estimate, increased from C$7.58B in Q3 and C$7.49B in Q4 2023.

Noninterest income of C$7.57B climbed from C$6.60B in the previous quarter and C$5.68B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of C$1.11B vs. C$1.07B in Q3 and C$878M in Q4 2023.

Total loans, net of allowance for losses, increased to C$940.5B from C$938.3B at July 31, 2024. Total deposits grew to C$1.27T from C$1.22T at the end of Q3.

Adjusted return on common equity was 11.7% vs. 14.1% in Q3 and 12.9% in Q4 2023.

Canadian Personal & Commercial Banking net income of C$1.82B fell 3% Q/Q and increased 9% Y/Y. Revenue of C$5.06B rose 1% Q/Q and 7% Y/Y.

U.S. Retail adjusted net income, including TD's investment in Charles Schwab (SCHW), was C$863B, slid 32% Y/Y. Net interest margin of 2.77% fell 25 basis points Q/Q, primarily from maintaining elevated liquidity levels.

Wealth Management & Insurance net income of C$349M dropped 19% Q/Q and 29% Y/Y; revenue of C$3.94B increased 18% Q/Q and 33% Y/Y.

Wholesale Banking generated Q4 revenue of C$1.77B, down 1% Q/Q and up 19% Y/Y; adjusted net income of C$299M sank 21% Q/Q and surged 68% Y/Y.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.

Earlier, TD Bank non-GAAP EPS of C$1.72, revenue of C$14.89B

data.title: Applied Materials slides on Morgan Stanley downgrade; sees 2025 as 'transition' year
data.description:
Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT) was in focus on Thursday as Morgan Stanley downgrade the semiconductor equipment firm, citing the belief 2025 will be a "transition" year for the wafer fab equipment market.

Applied Materials shares fell 2% in premarket trading as it was cut to Underweight from Equal-Weight and the price target was lowered to $179 from $164.

"Following record [wafer fab equipment] in 2024 lead by DRAM and China, we forecast 2025 to be a transition year," analysts at the firm wrote. "The longer-term secular drivers of [wafer fab equipment] market growth are intact as supply chain diversification remains top of mind and semiconductor capital intensity is not declining. However, end-markets matter and so does equipment purchase timing, and with declining DRAM ASP and significant pull-forward of Western equipment by Chinese players, we expect 2025 to be a transition year as companies digest capacity build from the prior year."

Overall, it sees wafer fab equipment spending down 6% year-over-year in 2025, with dynamic random access memory-related spending likely down 10% (despite 20% growth from high-bandwidth memory); NAND-related spending up 22%; spending from China down 15% (a 32% increase for Chinese companies compared to a 24% decline for Western companies); and foundry logic down roughly 6%.

"We forecast combined capex from [Taiwan Semiconductor], Intel, and Samsung to decline 2% [year-over-year] in 2025, but we have long-term questions around the foundry aspirations of Intel and Samsung," the analysts added.

The firm also tweaked its price targets on KLA Corp. (KLAC) and Lam Research (LRCX) to $703 and $78 from $746 and $76, respectively.
